title: cupressus karnaliensis subsp. mustangensis
intro: Cupressus karnaliensis subsp. mustangensis, also known as Himalayan Cypress, is a plant species endemic to the Mustang region of Nepal. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils such as bornyl acetate and alpha-pinene. The health benefits of Himalayan Cypress include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce anxiety. Himalayan Cypress can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used topically for skin issues and internally for respiratory health.
